title: Ancient Memphis: A Walk Through the White Walls
period: Early Dynastic / Old Kingdom, provisional research scope
summary: A compact living-history walk from the Nile edge through an inhabited district toward a reconstructed temple space.

stops.title: Nile Arrival
stops.summary: The visitor begins beside the river approach, with boats, reeds, workers, and the city edge ahead.
referenceNotes: Nile access is essential to Memphis, but this exact landing composition is provisional.

stops.title: Mudbrick Street
stops.summary: A narrow residential street uses mudbrick massing, plastered surfaces, courtyards, storage jars, and shade cloth.
referenceNotes: Domestic architecture is inferred from Egyptian settlement archaeology and comparable period material culture.

stops.title: Craftsmen Area
stops.summary: Pottery, basketry, stone dressing, and low workshop structures make the district feel economically alive.
referenceNotes: The specific placement of workshops in this compact route is artistic reconstruction until site-specific evidence is selected.

stops.title: Temple Exterior
stops.summary: A ceremonial exterior marks the transition from city life into sacred architecture.
referenceNotes: The final temple form depends on Step 2 research into the best Memphis temple anchor.

stops.title: Painted Interior
stops.summary: Painted walls, columns, incense, and ritual ambience show the intended reconstruction language.
referenceNotes: Interior paint is a product goal, but specific iconography must be evidence-reviewed before production use.
